task_completion.description: Open the raw FITS file in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing application (e.g., GIMP) is open and displaying the '656nmos.fits' file showing the Eagle Nebula.

task_completion.description: Crop the image to 500x500 pixels at the specified coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crop tool is used with dimensions set to 500x500 and an offset of X=550, Y=550, or the canvas size is visibly reduced to focus tightly on the core pillars.

task_completion.description: Adjust brightness and contrast to make pillar structures visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A brightness/contrast or levels/curves adjustment dialog is visible, and the image updates to show clear, distinct pillar structures compared to the faint raw FITS data.
The image colors are inverted, resulting in a white or very light background with the stars and nebula structures appearing dark/black.

task_completion.description: Add a visual marker and text annotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visual marker (e.g., a drawn arrow, circle, or crosshair) is placed at the tip of the largest central pillar, and the text 'M16 Core' is typed onto the image.

task_completion.description: Export the final finding chart as a PNG to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog shows the file being saved as 'm16_finding_chart.png' in the '~/AstroImages/finding_charts/output/' directory, or a file manager/terminal confirms the file's creation.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI image editor to perform the tasks rather than relying solely on terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical image editor (like GIMP) with visible toolbars, menus, and canvas manipulation, rather than just a terminal running Python or ImageMagick commands.

integrity.description: The agent manually applied the edits to the raw FITS file rather than downloading a pre-made finding chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Progressive steps of editing (cropping, adjusting contrast, inverting, adding text) are visible in the GUI, rather than a web browser being used to search for and download an existing 'M16 inverted finding chart'.

integrity.description: The agent exported the final result as a flattened image file, not a project file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog explicitly shows the .png extension being selected and saved, rather than an editor-specific project format (like .xcf or .psd).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The raw FITS file is located at '/home/ga/AstroImages/finding_charts/raw/656nmos.fits'. The expected output must be saved to '/home/ga/AstroImages/finding_charts/output/m16_finding_chart.png' with exact dimensions of 500x500 pixels. The final image must be inverted (dark stars/nebula on a white background) and include the text 'M16 Core'.
